prompts: The human skeletal system consists of 206 bones and is divided into two main parts: the axial and appendicular skeletons. The axial skeleton includes the skull, vertebral column, and rib cage, providing support and protection for the brain, spinal cord, and vital organs. The appendicular skeleton comprises the limbs and girdles: the pectoral girdle (shoulders), pelvic girdle (hips), and the bones of the arms and legs. Key bones include the humerus, radius, ulna, femur, tibia, and fibula. The skeletal system facilitates movement, supports the body, protects organs, stores minerals, and houses marrow for blood cell production.
